summary: Ukrainian drones are increasingly striking rear areas at distances of 40–100 km, with loitering munitions now operating far beyond the front line and combining cheap mass-produced models with expensive foreign analogues. Russian forces have intercepted Ukrainian UAVs using kinetic anti-drone systems like "Elka" and "Yolka," with the latter successfully engaging targets at 1,300 meters despite challenging sun-facing launches. Meanwhile, Ukrainian operators are countering Russian FPV drone attacks with electronic warfare interference and drone detectors, while new Ukrainian systems like the XDOWN STUD and AI-powered ZALA Z-16 are enhancing strike and reconnaissance capabilities.
